Migration trees are validated at generation time: bad filenames and
duplicate versions inside a stream are positioned wire errors, and the
embed pattern must cover the tree.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/migrations v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/migrations => MIGRATIONSDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- auth/migrations.go --
package auth

import "embed"

//fabrik:migrations
//go:embed all:migrations
var Migrations embed.FS
-- auth/migrations/not-a-migration.sql --
SELECT 1;
-- billing/migrations.go --
package billing

import "embed"

//fabrik:migrations
//go:embed all:migrations
var Migrations embed.FS
-- billing/migrations/0001_a.sql --
SELECT 1;
-- billing/migrations/0001_b.sql --
SELECT 1;
-- web/migrations.go --
package web

import "embed"

//fabrik:migrations
//go:embed migrations
var Plain embed.FS

//fabrik:migrations dir=files stream=bad//name
var NoEmbed embed.FS
-- web/migrations/0001_ok.sql --
SELECT 1;
-- want/diags --
error: web/migrations.go:5:1: the go:embed pattern does not cover all:migrations
  help: use //go:embed all:migrations - a plain pattern can silently omit migration files, and a migration that never ships never runs
error: web/migrations.go:9:1: //fabrik:migrations variable has no //go:embed
  help: add //go:embed all:files above the variable
error: web/migrations.go:9:38: invalid stream name: "bad//name" has a "" segment
  help: use clean slash-separated segments: stream=auth, stream=internal/billing
